types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequest

fields.name: client_secret
fields.description: Ephemeral key returned by the API.

fields.name: modalities
fields.description:
The set of modalities the model can respond with. To disable audio,
set this to ["text"].

fields.name: instructions
fields.description:
The default system instructions (i.e. system message) prepended to model calls. This field allows the client to guide the model on desired responses. The model can be instructed on response content and format, (e.g. "be extremely succinct", "act friendly", "here are examples of good responses") and on audio behavior (e.g. "talk quickly", "inject emotion into your voice", "laugh frequently"). The instructions are not guaranteed to be followed by the model, but they provide guidance to the model on the desired behavior.
Note that the server sets default instructions which will be used if this field is not set and are visible in the `session.created` event at the start of the session.

fields.name: input_audio_format
fields.description: The format of input audio. Options are `pcm16`, `g711_ulaw`, or `g711_alaw`.

fields.name: output_audio_format
fields.description: The format of output audio. Options are `pcm16`, `g711_ulaw`, or `g711_alaw`.

fields.name: input_audio_transcription
fields.description:
Configuration for input audio transcription, defaults to off and can be
set to `null` to turn off once on. Input audio transcription is not native
to the model, since the model consumes audio directly. Transcription runs
asynchronously and should be treated as rough guidance
rather than the representation understood by the model.

fields.name: speed
fields.description:
The speed of the model's spoken response. 1.0 is the default speed. 0.25 is
the minimum speed. 1.5 is the maximum speed. This value can only be changed
in between model turns, not while a response is in progress.

fields.name: tracing
fields.description:
Configuration options for tracing. Set to null to disable tracing. Once
tracing is enabled for a session, the configuration cannot be modified.

`auto` will create a trace for the session with default values for the
workflow name, group id, and metadata.

fields.name: turn_detection
fields.description:
Configuration for turn detection. Can be set to `null` to turn off. Server
VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on
audio volume and respond at the end of user speech.

fields.name: tools
fields.description: Tools (functions) available to the model.

fields.name: tool_choice
fields.description:
How the model chooses tools. Options are `auto`, `none`, `required`, or
specify a function.

fields.name: temperature
fields.description: Sampling temperature for the model, limited to [0.6, 1.2]. Defaults to 0.8.

fields.name: max_response_output_tokens
fields.description:
Maximum number of output tokens for a single assistant response,
inclusive of tool calls. Provide an integer between 1 and 4096 to
limit output tokens, or `inf` for the maximum available tokens for a
given model. Defaults to `inf`.

types.description:
A new Realtime session configuration, with an ephemeral key. Default TTL
for keys is one minute.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestClientSecret

fields.name: value
fields.description:
Ephemeral key usable in client environments to authenticate connections
to the Realtime API. Use this in client-side environments rather than
a standard API token, which should only be used server-side.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description:
Timestamp for when the token expires. Currently, all tokens expire
after one minute.

types.description: Ephemeral key returned by the API.

types.name: VoiceIdsOrCustomVoice
types.description: A built-in voice name or a custom voice reference.

types.name: VoiceIdsOrCustomVoiceVariant1

fields.name: id
fields.description: The custom voice ID, e.g. `voice_1234`.

types.description: Custom voice reference.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestInputAudioTranscription

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model to use for transcription.

types.description:
Configuration for input audio transcription, defaults to off and can be
set to `null` to turn off once on. Input audio transcription is not native
to the model, since the model consumes audio directly. Transcription runs
asynchronously and should be treated as rough guidance
rather than the representation understood by the model.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestTracing
types.description:
Configuration options for tracing. Set to null to disable tracing. Once
tracing is enabled for a session, the configuration cannot be modified.

`auto` will create a trace for the session with default values for the
workflow name, group id, and metadata.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestTracingTracingConfiguration

fields.name: workflow_name
fields.description:
The name of the workflow to attach to this trace. This is used to
name the trace in the traces dashboard.

fields.name: group_id
fields.description:
The group id to attach to this trace to enable filtering and
grouping in the traces dashboard.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description:
The arbitrary metadata to attach to this trace to enable
filtering in the traces dashboard.

types.description: Granular configuration for tracing.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestTurnDetection

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of turn detection, only `server_vad` is currently supported.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description:
Activation threshold for VAD (0.0 to 1.0), this defaults to 0.5. A
higher threshold will require louder audio to activate the model, and
thus might perform better in noisy environments.

fields.name: prefix_padding_ms
fields.description:
Amount of audio to include before the VAD detected speech (in
milliseconds). Defaults to 300ms.

fields.name: silence_duration_ms
fields.description:
Duration of silence to detect speech stop (in milliseconds). Defaults
to 500ms. With shorter values the model will respond more quickly,
but may jump in on short pauses from the user.

types.description:
Configuration for turn detection. Can be set to `null` to turn off. Server
VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on
audio volume and respond at the end of user speech.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool, i.e. `function`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
The description of the function, including guidance on when and how
to call it, and guidance about what to tell the user when calling
(if anything).

fields.name: parameters
fields.description: Parameters of the function in JSON Schema.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateRequestMaxResponseOutputTokens
types.description:
Maximum number of output tokens for a single assistant response,
inclusive of tool calls. Provide an integer between 1 and 4096 to
limit output tokens, or `inf` for the maximum available tokens for a
given model. Defaults to `inf`.

types.name: RealtimeTruncation
types.description:
When the number of tokens in a conversation exceeds the model's input token limit, the conversation be truncated, meaning messages (starting from the oldest) will not be included in the model's context. A 32k context model with 4,096 max output tokens can only include 28,224 tokens in the context before truncation occurs.

Clients can configure truncation behavior to truncate with a lower max token limit, which is an effective way to control token usage and cost.

Truncation will reduce the number of cached tokens on the next turn (busting the cache), since messages are dropped from the beginning of the context. However, clients can also configure truncation to retain messages up to a fraction of the maximum context size, which will reduce the need for future truncations and thus improve the cache rate.

Truncation can be disabled entirely, which means the server will never truncate but would instead return an error if the conversation exceeds the model's input token limit.

types.name: RealtimeTruncationString
types.description: The truncation strategy to use for the session. `auto` is the default truncation strategy. `disabled` will disable truncation and emit errors when the conversation exceeds the input token limit.

types.name: RealtimeTruncationRetentionRatio

fields.name: type
fields.description: Use retention ratio truncation.

fields.name: retention_ratio
fields.description: Fraction of post-instruction conversation tokens to retain (`0.0` - `1.0`) when the conversation exceeds the input token limit. Setting this to `0.8` means that messages will be dropped until 80% of the maximum allowed tokens are used. This helps reduce the frequency of truncations and improve cache rates.

fields.name: token_limits
fields.description: Optional custom token limits for this truncation strategy. If not provided, the model's default token limits will be used.

types.description: Retain a fraction of the conversation tokens when the conversation exceeds the input token limit. This allows you to amortize truncations across multiple turns, which can help improve cached token usage.

types.name: RealtimeTruncationRetentionRatioTokenLimits

fields.name: post_instructions
fields.description: Maximum tokens allowed in the conversation after instructions (which including tool definitions). For example, setting this to 5,000 would mean that truncation would occur when the conversation exceeds 5,000 tokens after instructions. This cannot be higher than the model's context window size minus the maximum output tokens.

types.description: Optional custom token limits for this truncation strategy. If not provided, the model's default token limits will be used.

types.name: Prompt

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier of the prompt template to use.

types.description:
Reference to a prompt template and its variables.
[Learn more](/docs/guides/text?api-mode=responses#reusable-prompts).

types.name: InputTextContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_text`.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text input to the model.

types.description: A text input to the model.

types.name: InputImageContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_image`.

types.description: An image input to the model. Learn about [image inputs](/docs/guides/vision).

types.name: InputFileContent

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_file`.

fields.name: filename
fields.description: The name of the file to be sent to the model.

fields.name: file_data
fields.description: The content of the file to be sent to the model.

fields.name: file_url
fields.description: The URL of the file to be sent to the model.

types.description: A file input to the model.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateResponse

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the session that looks like `sess_1234567890abcdef`.

fields.name: object
fields.description: The object type. Always `realtime.session`.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: Expiration timestamp for the session, in seconds since epoch.

fields.name: include
fields.description:
Additional fields to include in server outputs.
- `item.input_audio_transcription.logprobs`: Include logprobs for input audio transcription.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The Realtime model used for this session.

fields.name: output_modalities
fields.description:
The set of modalities the model can respond with. To disable audio,
set this to ["text"].

fields.name: instructions
fields.description:
The default system instructions (i.e. system message) prepended to model
calls. This field allows the client to guide the model on desired
responses. The model can be instructed on response content and format,
(e.g. "be extremely succinct", "act friendly", "here are examples of good
responses") and on audio behavior (e.g. "talk quickly", "inject emotion
into your voice", "laugh frequently"). The instructions are not guaranteed
to be followed by the model, but they provide guidance to the model on the
desired behavior.

Note that the server sets default instructions which will be used if this
field is not set and are visible in the `session.created` event at the
start of the session.

fields.name: audio
fields.description: Configuration for input and output audio for the session.

fields.name: tracing
fields.description:
Configuration options for tracing. Set to null to disable tracing. Once
tracing is enabled for a session, the configuration cannot be modified.

`auto` will create a trace for the session with default values for the
workflow name, group id, and metadata.

fields.name: turn_detection
fields.description:
Configuration for turn detection. Can be set to `null` to turn off. Server
VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on
audio volume and respond at the end of user speech.

fields.name: tools
fields.description: Tools (functions) available to the model.

fields.name: tool_choice
fields.description:
How the model chooses tools. Options are `auto`, `none`, `required`, or
specify a function.

fields.name: max_output_tokens
fields.description:
Maximum number of output tokens for a single assistant response,
inclusive of tool calls. Provide an integer between 1 and 4096 to
limit output tokens, or `inf` for the maximum available tokens for a
given model. Defaults to `inf`.

types.description: A Realtime session configuration object.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateResponseAudio
types.description: Configuration for input and output audio for the session.

fields.name: noise_reduction
fields.description: Configuration for input audio noise reduction.

fields.name: turn_detection
fields.description: Configuration for turn detection.

types.name: RealtimeAudioFormatsAudioPcm

fields.name: type
fields.description: The audio format. Always `audio/pcm`.

fields.name: rate
fields.description: The sample rate of the audio. Always `24000`.

types.description: The PCM audio format. Only a 24kHz sample rate is supported.

types.name: RealtimeAudioFormatsAudioPcmu

fields.name: type
fields.description: The audio format. Always `audio/pcmu`.

types.description: The G.711 μ-law format.

types.name: RealtimeAudioFormatsAudioPcma

fields.name: type
fields.description: The audio format. Always `audio/pcma`.

types.description: The G.711 A-law format.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The model used for transcription. Current options are `whisper-1`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`, `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`, and `gpt-realtime-whisper`.

fields.name: language
fields.description: The language of the input audio.

fields.name: prompt
fields.description: The prompt configured for input audio transcription, when present.

types.name: AudioTranscriptionResponseModel
types.description: The model used for transcription. Current options are `whisper-1`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe-2025-12-15`, `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize`, and `gpt-realtime-whisper`.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateResponseAudioInputNoiseReduction
types.description: Configuration for input audio noise reduction.

types.name: NoiseReductionType
types.description: Type of noise reduction. `near_field` is for close-talking microphones such as headphones, `far_field` is for far-field microphones such as laptop or conference room microphones.

types.name: RealtimeSessionCreateResponseAudioInputTurnDetection

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of turn detection, only `server_vad` is currently supported.

types.description: Configuration for turn detection.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the tool, i.e. `function`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the function.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
The description of the function, including guidance on when and how
to call it, and guidance about what to tell the user when calling
(if anything).

fields.name: parameters
fields.description: Parameters of the function in JSON Schema.

methods.description:
Create an ephemeral API token for use in client-side applications with the
Realtime API. Can be configured with the same session parameters as the
`session.update` client event.

It responds with a session object, plus a `client_secret` key which contains
a usable ephemeral API token that can be used to authenticate browser clients
for the Realtime API.

Returns the created Realtime session object, plus an ephemeral key.

requestBody.description: Create an ephemeral API key with the given session configuration.
